customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety, high agency. Benjamin is a motivated buyer running a structured comparison process across multiple solar companies. He is not skeptical of solar — he is managing his own decision timeline. The risk is not resistance; it is that he books with a competitor before Tuesday if Ion does not differentiate.
unresolved_concerns: Benjamin expects to receive 'information in the packet' by email before the appointment — this was never addressed or resolved. If no packet arrives, he may interpret it as Ion being unresponsive.

Benjamin floated a promotional partnership concept (Ion installs as a learning/demo project in exchange for community exposure). This was not acknowledged and the closer does not know about it. It may reflect how Benjamin is thinking about the transaction.

Benjamin is comparing multiple companies and plans to make his decision 'at the end of the week.' The appointment is Tuesday — the closer is competing against a decision deadline, not just other companies.
sensitive_topics_raised: LLC / nonprofit transition — the property is being used as an educational center, not a standard residence. The closer should confirm whether Ion's residential program applies or whether this requires a different product path.
Grid reliability / outage protection — Benjamin's primary stated motivation. Not a sensitive topic in the emotional sense, but critical context the closer must lead with.
exact_promises_rep_made: Ion will build a design for the home and have a specialist call Tuesday at 9AM.
The specialist will call (phone appointment, not in-person visit).
The closer's name was not provided — Meg said she would 'figure out who will be calling.' No text follow-up was explicitly promised, though Meg referenced sending appointment details by email.
closer_should_know: Benjamin's primary motivation is grid reliability and outage protection — he explicitly mentioned power outages in the mountains. Lead with Powerwall backup capability, not cost savings. He is also running a structured comparison across multiple companies with a self-imposed end-of-week decision deadline; the closer needs to differentiate Ion on the first call, not assume a second chance.
